div1.entryFree: Mate, vb. to confound, to paralyze, to disable: her more than haste is --d with delays, Ven. 909. not mad, but --d, Err. III, 2, 54 (double meaning). I think you are all --d or stark mad, V, 281. that is good deceit which --s him first that first intends deceit, H6B III, 1, 265. my mind she has --d, and amazed my sight, Mcb. V, 1, 86.
